prose:
Computed Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde

Method-provenance block
• Property: condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰ (radical attack = ½(f⁺+f⁻)), and dual descriptor (f⁺–f⁻) on each atom of H₂C=O; headline value below is the largest f⁺.
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF (called via chemkit).
• Geometry provenance: fixed experimental Cartesian geometry supplied by user file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz; no further optimisation (single-point charges/energies).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles converged (integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true). No `warnings` array present; integrity checks (see below) all passed.
• Live engine log: calculation ran via chemkit’s “fukui” skill (engine subcommand `fukui`). The live PySCF output streamed to the standard `<cwd>/fukui_<timestamp>.out` file; path was announced during execution and is retained on disk alongside the JSON result /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpt0yk157b/live.json.

Key numerical results (Mulliken partition):
Atom (labeling follows input order O1 C2 H3 H4)
  f⁺  = [0.46813824, 0.70839130, –0.088269392, –0.088260145]
  f⁻  = [0.46742464, 0.11244422,  0.21007563,  0.21005551]
  f⁰  = [0.46778144, 0.41041776,  0.060903121, 0.060897683]
  Dual descriptor (f⁺–f⁻) = [0.00071359555, 0.59594709, –0.29834503, –0.29831566]

Most electrophilic site: C2 (index 1) with f⁺ = 0.70839130
Most nucleophilic site: O1 (index 0) with f⁻ = 0.46742464

All fukui values correctly sum to ≈1.0 for both f⁺ and f⁻, confirming charge conservation.

• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
